chief_complaint: A 56-year-old man with a long-standing HIV infection presents with a diagnosis of locally advanced anal squamous cell carcinoma.
history: The patient was diagnosed with locally advanced anal squamous cell carcinoma (T4N1cM0) and received standard concurrent chemoradiotherapy (CRT) with mitomycin C and 5-fluorouracil (5-FU). He experienced complications including Grade 3 febrile leukopenia, Grade 2 radiodermatitis, and scrotal lymphedema. The patient's HIV infection is well-managed on stable antiretroviral therapy (ART).
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: Imaging and endoscopic evaluation revealed fibrotic changes without evidence of active disease at six months post-treatment. Retrospective immunohistochemical analysis of the original tumor revealed strong p16 expression, aberrant p53 expression, and a markedly elevated Ki-67 index (99%).
correct_diagnosis: Locally advanced anal squamous cell carcinoma with aggressive tumor biology
differentials: Locally advanced anal squamous cell carcinoma with aggressive tumor biology
Basal cell carcinoma of the anus
Squamous cell carcinoma of the anal margin
correct_ranking: Locally advanced anal squamous cell carcinoma with aggressive tumor biology
Basal cell carcinoma of the anus
Squamous cell carcinoma of the anal margin
